title: KDB Compile Result (page-intents model)
description: Aggregate LLM output across all sources in a compile run. The LLM emits semantic intent only: slugs, titles, bodies, logical links. Python owns paths, frontmatter, timestamps, versions, incoming-link reconciliation. See docs/CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md D8 and KDB/KDB-Compiler-System-Prompt.md.

run_id.description: Stamped by Python (run_context.py). LLM does not emit run_ids.

log_entries.description: LLM-emitted log items (contradictions, ambiguities, low-confidence decisions). Persisted in state/runs/<run_id>.json journal.

canonical_meta.description: Stage 6 (canonicalize) output (#74). Absent on raw extraction (pre-Stage 6); present post-Stage 6. Optional per D-R5-7 so the same schema validates both pre- and post-canonicalized payloads.

slug.description: Lowercase ASCII kebab-case identifier. No paths, no .md suffixes, no directory separators. The 'summary-' prefix is reserved (see summarySlug).

summarySlug.description: Slug for the per-source summary page. MUST start with the reserved 'summary-' prefix followed by the kebab-cased source filename stem.

sourceId.description: Canonical relative path of a raw source (set by Python; the LLM echoes back the one it was given).

pageType.description: Only LLM-authored page types. Index and log are Python-authored and not emittable by the LLM.

pageIntent.description: One page the LLM wants to create or replace. Full-body replacement model (D18).

body.description: Full markdown body, no frontmatter. Python prepends frontmatter during apply.

supports_page_existence.description: source_ids that justify this page existing. Must reference real sources (current or manifest-known).

outgoing_links.description: Slugs this page's body links to. Must appear in body as [[slug]]. Python reconciles incoming_links_known from these.

canonical_id.description: Stage 6 (canonicalize) output (#74). NULL means this page is canonical; a non-null slug means this page is an alias of the referenced canonical (D-R5-5). Optional per D-R5-7 — absent on raw extraction; populated post-Stage 6.

pages.description: Every page the LLM wants to create or replace on behalf of this source (summary + concepts + articles).

compile_meta.description: Per-source model-call metadata stamped by Python. Present only when this entry came from a live compile; omitted for fixture-backed compile_result.json. Optional at this level so M1.7 fixture tests remain valid.

canonicalMeta.description: Stage 6 canonicalization output (#74). Records every alias resolution, link remap, and page merge that happened so `graphdb-kdb rebuild` can reconstruct the same state from this run's sidecar.

algorithm_version.description: Version of the canonicalize algorithm. Bumps when the algorithm changes in a way that affects output; replay tolerates older versions iff backward-compatible.

ledger_snapshot_sha256.description: sha256 of the aliases.json bytes at compile time (D-R5-7). Sentinel 'empty' when the ledger was absent. Rebuild verifies this matches the current ledger before applying aliases.

aliases_emitted.description: Aliases actually used in this run (resolved from a page-slug, outgoing_link, or body wikilink position). Drives graph_sync alias-Entity row creation in #74.5.

algorithm.description: Mechanism that produced this alias mapping. v1 emits 'ledger' only; v2 gates add 'embedding' and 'llm_judge'.

outgoing_link_remaps.description: Deduped (from, to) pairs of every link-position remap (outgoing_links metadata + body [[wikilink]] tokens).

merged_pages.description: Per-merge log for OQ-F page-intent merges. One entry per alias page intent that was folded into a canonical.
